questions.question: In a game of repeated Prisoner's Dilemma, the dominant strategy for both players is to always cooperate, as this leads to the highest expected payoff. Explain why this is true.
questions.hint: Consider the payoffs for each player

questions.question: A game of repeated Chicken has a saddle point where both players choose to stay, resulting in a suboptimal outcome. Explain why this occurs and discuss a potential strategy for players to improve their payoff.
questions.hint: Consider the Nash equilibrium and the concept of a saddle point

questions.question: A game of repeated Auction has a unique Nash equilibrium where both players choose to bid equally. Explain why this is the case and discuss a potential strategy for one player to deviate from this equilibrium.
questions.hint: Consider the concept of a Nash equilibrium and the payoffs for each player
